content.verdict: COMO Uma Paro is the smartest-priced luxury option in Bhutan — 29 rooms and 9 villas spread across a hilltop pine forest above Paro, anchored by the COMO Shambhala spa. For honeymooners who want Aman-level wellness and Tiger's Nest access without Aman pricing, this is the answer. The villas in particular punch well above their nightly rate.
content.best_room: Book a One-Bedroom Villa with private garden, fireplace, and outdoor hot tub — these are the honeymoon-defining rooms at COMO Uma. The Como Villa with two bedrooms is overkill for two but tempting if you're traveling with friends. Standard rooms are lovely but the villa upgrade is the right play here.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Paro
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Paro airport, transfer 10 minutes uphill to COMO Uma. Welcome juice, settle into your villa, in-suite hot stone bath, dinner at Bukhari, the lodge's flagship restaurant in a circular fire-warmed pavilion.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tiger's Nest hike
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn breakfast and drive to Taktsang trailhead. The 4-5 hour hike to the cliffside monastery defines the trip. Picnic at the cafeteria viewpoint. Return for an extended COMO Shambhala massage and a soak in your villa hot tub.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Paro dzong and town
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Rinpung Dzong and the National Museum housed in the watchtower above. Afternoon walk through Paro's main street market — yathra fabrics, prayer flags, and incense. Archery lesson at the lodge in the late afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day trip to Thimphu
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 1.5 hours to the capital. Visit the giant Buddha Dordenma, Tashichho Dzong at sunset, and the weekend market if you're there Saturday or Sunday. Dinner back at COMO.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Drukpath day-hike
itinerary_7_nights.description: Section-hike of the Drukpath trail through blue pine forest and rhododendron groves. Picnic at a high alpine lake. Sunset prayer flag tying ceremony.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wellness day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Sunrise yoga in the COMO Shambhala pavilion, signature couples Bhutanese-inspired treatment, hot stone bath. Afternoon at leisure — the library has a curated selection of Himalaya books. Dinner in your villa by the fire.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on the villa terrace, transfer back to Paro airport for the early-morning Druk Air departure.

caveats: Bhutan visa requires a licensed tour operator — COMO partners with Yangphel Adventure Travel for this; allow 4-6 weeks.
Sustainable Development Fee of $200 per person per night applies on top of the room rate.
Standard rooms are lovely but the villas are the honeymoon experience — budget the upgrade.
Internet is functional but not fast — bring offline-loaded entertainment for evenings.

faqs.question: Is COMO Uma Paro good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Excellent value for the level. The villa rooms with private fireplaces and hot tubs are equal to anything Aman or Six Senses offer at noticeably lower nightly rates, with the celebrated COMO Shambhala spa on site.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March-May for rhododendrons, September-November for the clearest mountain views and festival season.

faqs.question: How far in advance should we book?
faqs.answer: Three to four months ahead is usually sufficient — COMO has more inventory than Aman or Six Senses, so it's an easier book.

faqs.question: Do we need a guide?
faqs.answer: Yes — Bhutanese law requires a licensed guide and driver, included in your COMO package via their tour-operator partner.

faqs.question: What's the best room type?
faqs.answer: One-Bedroom Villa, every time. Private garden, fireplace, outdoor hot tub — the right honeymoon room at this property.

faqs.question: How do we get to COMO Uma Paro?
faqs.answer: Fly Druk Air or Bhutan Airlines into Paro. COMO arranges the 10-minute uphill transfer.
